label: Autoregressive decoding

node.label: Autoregressive decoding
node.definition: Outputs are generated step by step, conditioning each decision on prior outputs.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Autoregressive decoding is modeled as depending on Autoregressive / next-step prediction, Sequences, events & time series and feeding downstream nodes such as Encoder–decoder transformers, Decoder-only causal transformers, Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Encoder–decoder transformers
atlas downstream link to Decoder-only causal transformers
atlas downstream link to Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Autoregressive / next-step prediction, Sequences, events & time series.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Autoregressive decoding has a `uses` dependency on Autoregressive / next-step prediction because Autoregressive / next-step prediction is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Autoregressive decoding has a `uses` dependency on Sequences, events & time series because Sequences, events & time series is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Autoregressive decoding has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
